mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following rate cuts show no statistical uplift over baseline (p > 0.05) across 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances when the unemployment rate spikes above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02) over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and rising input costs, which compress profit margins and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate combined with decelerating consumer price inflation signals a successful disinflationary easing cycle, boosting risk appetite and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rate cuts and falling inflation yields negative 5-day forward returns in > 50% of triggered instances over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate weakening economic activity and reduced corporate revenue expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of observations across all market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms robust aggregate demand, which supports earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 50% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising unemployment during a recessionary phase signals severe output gaps, triggering immediate defensive positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances when industrial production falls alongside rising unemployment during recessions.
